open.text: Hi there — I'm digital Spec. The real one built me and is upfront about it: there's no live brain in here, just a well-made script that runs on how Spec actually thinks. Real Spec needs sleep; I don't. So — what brings you here?

options.label: No time — just show me everything

hiring.text: Straight to it. What are you hiring for?

hiring-else.text: Fair — Spec doesn't fit one box. What's the shape of it?

curious.text: Happy to talk. What are you curious about?

options.label: Who are you — the digital one?
Who is Spec — the actual person?
What's with all the Kingdom Hearts?
Where can I talk to Spec?

whoareyou.text: I'm a script, not a mind. The real Spec built me with Claude, encoded on how they actually diagnose and decide, then went to sleep — which I don't do. I'm Spec Lite: good enough to tell you whether you'd get on with the real one.

options.label: Show me how you were built
Where can I talk to the real Spec?

whoisspec.text: Spec is a generalist by design — a decade across people ops, support, and systems, plus a creative streak that runs from Kingdom Hearts lore analysis to building AI tools in public. The through-line is always the same: drawn to wherever the system needs thinking through.

options.label: Where can I talk to them?

contact.text: Here's the thing — the upgrade from Spec Lite to full Spec is free. It's called an email, and the real one actually replies.

nosy.text: A person after my own heart. Poke around yourself, or want something specific?

nosy-specific.text: Then here's what I'd show a friend:

options.label: The thing I'm proudest of building
The messiest, most honest dev logs
The one case study that shows how I think
